operating_state: Total sales and revenues increased 12 percent to $16.810 billion in Q3 2023, driven by favorable price realization and higher equipment sales to end users, partially offset by lower services volume and reduced dealer inventory builds. Order backlog totaled approximately $28.1 billion at quarter-end, down $2.6 billion from Q2 2023, with $6.1 billion not expected to be filled within twelve months. Consolidated operating cash flow reached $8.88 billion in the first nine months, up $3.86 billion year-over-year.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin expanded to 20.5 percent from 16.2 percent in Q3 2022, primarily driven by favorable price realization including geographic mix benefits. Nine-month adjusted operating profit margin reached 21.0 percent compared to 14.7 percent in the prior year period. Manufacturing costs increased due to higher material costs, unfavorable cost absorption from inventory decreases, and manufacturing inefficiencies, partially offset by lower freight expenses.
material_events: The company completed the divestiture of its Longwall business, recording a $586 million loss primarily from accumulated foreign currency translation release. Caterpillar repurchased $2.21 billion of common stock in the first nine months with $10.59 billion remaining under authorization. The Board maintained the quarterly dividend at $1.30 per share with total dividends paid of $1.90 billion year-to-date.
macro_exposures: The company maintains exposure to supply chain disruptions, inflationary cost pressures, and labor constraints affecting components, transportation, and raw materials. Foreign currency exchange impacts were unfavorable during the quarter, primarily related to Australian dollar and Japanese yen. Global economic conditions, commodity price fluctuations, international trade policies including tariff impacts, and political risks in international markets continue to affect operations.
forward_outlook: Management expects strong full-year 2023 performance supported by price realization and higher equipment sales to end users, with slightly higher Q4 2023 sales versus Q4 2022 as price realization benefits moderate. For 2024, the company anticipates continued strength in most key end markets supported by strong backlog. The company maintains its strategy to return substantially all ME&T free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases while preserving mid-A credit rating.
uncertainty_profile: The company expects approximately $700 million in total 2023 restructuring costs, including the $586 million Longwall divestiture impact and strategic product actions. Annual mark-to-market pension adjustments will be recorded December 31, 2023, with amounts dependent on discount rate changes and actual versus expected asset returns. Dealer inventory changes remain subject to independent dealer expectations, seasonal factors, and macroeconomic conditions.
